metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether the emitted binding walk census log key should be renamed from 'binding_walk_census' to 'decision_census' (or update AC #2 and Phase-2 alerting specs to reference 'binding_walk_census'), so the plan's event-key assertions match the actual emit in run_differs.py:535.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'decision_census emitted every pass in EVERY mode' (Remaining CODE section and AC #2), but the actual emit in run_differs.py:535 uses the event key 'binding_walk_census', not 'decision_census'. The census() docstring in classify.py claims the emit key is 'decision_census' but this is doc-only — the real call site uses a different name.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section ('decision_census emitted every pass in EVERY mode'); AC #2 ('decision_census emitted every pass in every mode'); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py:535; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/classify.py:351
findings.suggested_fix: Either update run_differs.py:535 to emit `sync_logger.log("decision_census", **walk.census)` to match the plan and classify.py docstring, or update the plan/AC to reference the real event key 'binding_walk_census' and ensure any alert wiring uses that name.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete description of what 'classifier-vs-legacy' means in code (which two code paths are compared, where the comparison runs, and what log event it emits), since no such comparison exists anywhere in the codebase today.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting in shadow (Phase-2 breadth)' as a remaining code deliverable, and AC #2 requires it. No implementation of this alerting exists anywhere in the codebase — no 'classifier_vs_legacy', no divergence comparison between classifier and legacy paths, no shadow-alerting for this dimension.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section ('classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting in shadow (Phase-2 breadth)'); AC #2 ('classifier-vs-legacy divergence logged + alerting in shadow')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the target file (e.g. `classify.py` or `baseline_shadow.py`), the function name, and the log key for the classifier-vs-legacy divergence alert so that AC #2 has a concrete edit target.
findings.finding: The 'classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting in shadow (Phase-2 breadth)' is listed as remaining code but no target file, function, or log-key name is specified in the plan. Zero code matches for 'classifier-vs-legacy' exist anywhere in the repo, and the plan provides no location for where this emit will live.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section: 'decision_census emitted every pass in EVERY mode + classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting in shadow (Phase-2 breadth)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the classifier-vs-legacy divergence alert event: name the `SyncLogger` event key, enumerate its fields (e.g. `classifier_count`, `legacy_count`, `divergent_pairs`, `pass_id`), and specify what 'legacy' refers to (the existing `binding_walk_census` path vs the new `decision_census` path).
findings.finding: AC #2 and the 'Remaining CODE' section reference 'classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting in shadow (Phase-2 breadth)' but neither the log event key, the event schema/fields, nor the definition of what constitutes 'legacy' vs 'classifier' output is specified anywhere in the plan or codebase.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section ('decision_census emitted every pass in EVERY mode + classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting in shadow'); AC #2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `decision_census` replaces `binding_walk_census` (rename + migrate consumers) or is emitted in addition to it, and document the final canonical event key in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan mandates `decision_census` emitted every pass, but the existing emit in `run_differs.py:535` uses the key `binding_walk_census`, and `classify.py:351-353` says the `decision_census` emit call 'lands with the first consumer' (i.e., not yet written). The plan does not resolve whether `binding_walk_census` is renamed, duplicated, or replaced.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section; AC #2; `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py:535`; `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/classify.py:351-353`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the new RED direction-preservation property test: name its target module (`outbound_fields._local_matches_prev` with `BindingStore.get_baseline`), the five mirrored fields it must cover, and how it differs from the existing `test_property_direction_preservation` in `test_properties.py`.
findings.finding: The plan requires a 'RED-first direction-preservation property test running against the SWAPPED consumer,' but `test_property_direction_preservation` already exists in `test_properties.py:153-167` and tests the pure `classify.direction_is_inbound` kernel — not the swapped `outbound_fields._local_matches_prev` consumer. The plan does not specify the new test's scope, fixture inputs, or how it differs from the existing one.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section ('RED-first direction-preservation property test running against the SWAPPED consumer')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the consecutive-pass counting rule for non-clean-pass outcomes: define that any GHA run producing no `baseline_shadow_check` log (exit 75, exit 3, timeout, or workflow failure) resets the counter to 0 and must be resolved before counting resumes, and document how the operator verifies continuity (e.g., check pass_id timestamps are ≤25 min apart across the 10-entry sequence).
findings.finding: The 10-consecutive-clean-pass gate relies on manual operator counting from sync logs, but the plan does not specify how a workflow failure mid-sequence (GHA exit 75/reschedule, timeout, exit 3/pass-in-flight) is treated — such a run produces no `baseline_shadow_check` log entry, so an operator's count could silently absorb a gap or be incorrectly maintained if they fail to notice the run was absent, allowing the Phase-3 flip to proceed on fewer than 10 actual clean comparisons.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section / AC line 3: '>= 10 consecutive clean live shadow passes'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Remaining OPERATIONAL section: 'A clean pass is defined by an unbroken sequence of 10 sync logs each containing `baseline_shadow_check divergent==0`; any gap in the sequence (no log within 25 min of the previous pass_id, or a log with divergent>0) resets the counter to 0. Verify continuity by confirming pass timestamps are ≤25 min apart across the 10-entry window.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the streak-tracking mechanism for the 10-consecutive-clean-pass gate: either a log query (with field names and reset condition), a workflow artifact/variable, or a dedicated script — and define the reset rule when a divergent pass interrupts the sequence.
findings.finding: The 10-consecutive-clean-shadow-pass gate has no specified mechanism for tracking or resetting the streak counter. No code, workflow variable, or log query is defined for counting consecutive clean passes, and the plan gives no rule for resetting the count when a divergent pass interrupts the sequence.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section ('>=10 consecutive clean live shadow passes'); AC #3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact feature-flag key name for the Phase-3 consumer swap in the plan (e.g. `reconciler.phase3_consumer_swap`) so both the implementing agent and the production operator can reference it unambiguously.
findings.finding: The Phase-3 consumer-swap flag name is never stated in the plan ('behind a flag' is used throughout without naming the flag). An agent implementing the code cannot proceed without inventing a name or stopping to ask.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section; AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to the 'Remaining CODE' section: 'Feature flag: `reconciler.phase3_consumer_swap` (bool, default false).' Repeat the key verbatim in AC1 and AC5.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the Phase-3 consumer-swap feature flag (e.g. `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap`) and add it to `ReconcilerConfig` in `_config_schema.py` so the plan references a concrete, schema-defined key.
findings.finding: The Phase-3 consumer-swap flag is described as 'behind a flag' throughout the plan but its config key name is never defined. `ReconcilerConfig` in `_config_schema.py` has only `baseline_dual_write`; no second flag exists. An implementer must invent a key name, breaking the producer↔consumer contract discipline the plan itself cites.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section; AC #1; AC #5
findings.suggested_fix: Add `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False` to `ReconcilerConfig` in `_config_schema.py` and replace every 'behind a flag' reference in the plan with `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap`.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the Phase-3 consumer-swap config flag explicitly in the plan (e.g. `reconciler.baseline_consumer`) and ensure it is distinct from the Phase-1 `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` flag, so AC1 and AC5 (rollback rehearsed) reference the same concrete knob.
findings.finding: The plan's Phase-3 consumer swap is described as 're-point `outbound_fields._local_matches_prev` from prev_snapshot to `BindingStore.get_baseline` behind a flag' but never names the flag. The Phase-1 shadow flag is `reconciler.baseline_dual_write`; the Phase-3 flip needs a distinct flag (e.g. `reconciler.baseline_consumer`). Without a stated flag name, an implementer must invent it, and the rollback procedure in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` cannot reference the correct flag — making the rollback step ('pin workflow mode to dry-run') the only recourse rather than the flag-based toggle.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section / AC line 1: 'Phase-3 consumer-swap code merged behind a flag'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete rollback-rehearsal step to 'Remaining OPERATIONAL' (e.g., 'In a staging/dry-run invocation, confirm that setting MODE=dry-run in the workflow env halts all mutations and that the previous shadow baseline is still readable') so AC #5 has a described work item to map to.
findings.finding: AC #5 requires rollback to be 'rehearsed' but the plan body contains no described work item or procedure for conducting the rehearsal — the rollback procedure is only noted as already documented in landed code, making this criterion element an orphan with no described work to map it to.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #5: 'rollback (pin workflow mode to dry-run) rehearsed'
findings.suggested_fix: In 'Remaining OPERATIONAL', add: 'Rollback rehearsal: trigger one manual workflow run with MODE=dry-run after the live heal; confirm zero mutations applied and baseline_shadow_check still emits; record run id as evidence for AC #5.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC1 (e.g. `pytest tests/test_direction_preservation.py -k phase3_consumer --tb=short`) and specify the expected RED→GREEN sequence so reviewers can verify the property test exercises the swapped consumer path.
findings.finding: AC1's direction-preservation property test has no concrete proving command. The plan says 'RED-first direction-preservation property test running against the SWAPPED consumer' but names no test file, no framework invocation, and no command that would produce pass/fail evidence.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section ('RED-first direction-preservation property test'); AC line 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit, justified acknowledgment that GC-after-grace and archived->Done transitions are irreversible, and specify the manual back-out path (e.g., Jira admin restore steps, or a pre-heal snapshot export) so the destructive step is an explicit justified choice with a named remediation.
findings.finding: The capped live heal includes a GC-after-grace deletion (f8b5-2f30->REB-530) which is a destructive/potentially irreversible Jira mutation, but the plan provides no explicit back-out path for already-applied mutations — the stated rollback ('pin workflow mode to dry-run') only prevents future mutations and does not address reverting Jira state changes already written during the heal pass.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict (MODE_CAPS=10) of the 7 known drift instances: ... f8b5-2f30->REB-530 (GC-after-grace)'
findings.suggested_fix: Before the live heal step, add: 'Pre-heal: export current Jira state of all 7 instances (JSON snapshot via Jira API) to a dated artifact. If a heal mutation misfires, restore from snapshot via Jira admin bulk-import. GC-after-grace on f8b5-2f30 is acknowledged irreversible; proceed only after snapshot is confirmed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the counter-reset policy for the >=10-consecutive-clean-shadow-pass gate: specify that any divergent pass resets the count to zero, that a baseline_shadow_divergence alert is emitted on reset, and that the gate log must show 10 unbroken consecutive pass IDs.
findings.finding: The >=10-consecutive-clean-shadow-pass gate (AC line 3) does not specify what happens when a divergent pass appears mid-sequence: whether the counter resets to zero, triggers an alert, or pauses the gate. This makes the gate ambiguous and potentially unsafe (an operator might manually count wrong or skip an alert).
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — '>= 10 consecutive clean live shadow passes'; AC line 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the operational procedure: 'Any pass with divergent>0 resets the consecutive counter to 0 and emits a baseline_shadow_divergence alert. The gate is satisfied only when 10 unbroken consecutive pass IDs (all divergent==0) are recorded in the log.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Prefix each AC with its responsible party, e.g. `[CODE/AGENT]` for ACs 1–2 and `[OPERATIONAL/OPERATOR]` for ACs 3–5, to make in-session vs post-deployment obligations explicit.
findings.finding: The five ACs are not labeled by responsible party (agent vs operator), even though the plan body explicitly separates 'agent-doable' code work from 'operator-only' operational work. An agent reading only the AC block cannot determine which ACs it is expected to close.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section (all 5 AC lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Prefix AC1 and AC2 with `[CODE]` and AC3–5 with `[OPERATIONAL]`, matching the section labels already used in the plan body.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the Phase-3 consumer-swap feature flag (e.g. `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap`) and enumerate its edit targets: `ReconcilerConfig` field + `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer in `_config_schema.py`, plus the call sites in `reconcile.py`/`run_differs.py` that will branch on it.
findings.finding: The Phase-3 consumer swap is described as going 'behind a flag', but no flag name is specified and no such config key exists yet in _config_schema.py ReconcilerConfig (only baseline_dual_write is there). The flag's addition to ReconcilerConfig, _SECTIONS['reconciler'], and any callers in reconcile.py / run_differs.py is an unenumerated edit target.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section: 'Phase-3 consumer swap … behind a flag'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'Flag name: `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False`. Edit targets: `_config_schema.py` ReconcilerConfig + _SECTIONS["reconciler"]; `run_differs.py` OutboundDiffConfig construction (pass `get_baseline(local_id)` instead of `prev_snapshot.get(jira_key)` when flag is set).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten AC5 to require a concrete rollback-rehearsal artifact: e.g. 'attach the workflow run-id of a forced dry-run pass executed after the flag flip, confirming the reconciler reverted to dry-run mode and emitted zero mutations.'
findings.finding: AC5's 'rollback (pin workflow mode to dry-run) rehearsed' has no concrete artifact or proving command. 'Rehearsed' is a one-time transition claim with no evidence mechanism, making it unverifiable after the fact.
findings.location: AC line 5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or a clearly-marked section in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`) capturing the Phase-3 decision: why `BindingStore.get_baseline` replaces `prev_snapshot` as the baseline source in `outbound_fields._local_matches_prev`, the flag name and its operational lifecycle, and the preserved degrade/fail-closed contracts.
findings.finding: The Phase-3 consumer swap — re-pointing `outbound_fields._local_matches_prev` (and `outbound_differ`) from `prev_snapshot` to `BindingStore.get_baseline` — is a novel architectural decision that changes the source-of-truth for baseline comparison in the acting path, but the plan mentions no new ADR or update to existing docs (e.g., `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`) to capture this decision. ADR 0026 is cited only for the direction-arbitration rules to be *preserved*, not for the swap itself.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section; AC line 1 ('Phase-3 consumer-swap code merged behind a flag')
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/0027-phase3-consumer-swap.md` (or append to ADR 0026 as a resolved amendment) with: (1) context — prev_snapshot limitation; (2) decision — swap to BindingStore.get_baseline behind flag `<flag_name>`; (3) consequences — absent-baseline local-wins degrade, corrupt-store fail-closed abort, prev_snapshot retained for L3 suppression; (4) operational gate — the 10-pass shadow requirement before flag flip.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the heal operations are idempotent (or add a pre-check guard) so that re-running the 20-min workflow against already-healed instances is a no-op, and document the expected behavior if the workflow fires during the heal window.
findings.finding: The 7-instance heal runs under the recurring 20-min workflow (MODE_CAPS=10), but the plan does not specify whether the heal operations are idempotent if the workflow fires again before the fixed-point assertion is confirmed. Re-healing already-Done or already-GC'd items could cause duplicate mutations or false-positive census counts.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict (MODE_CAPS=10) of the 7 known drift instances'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a precondition check in the heal step: before issuing each mutation, verify the target state is not already reached (e.g., ticket not already Done/GC'd/adopted). Document that the bootstrap-strict cap (MODE_CAPS=10) still applies to re-runs, so partial re-heals are bounded even if not perfectly idempotent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document explicitly whether the live heal mutations (archived->Done, GC-after-grace, adopt) are considered permanent and acceptable regardless of flag rollback, or specify a manual Jira revert procedure; update the rollback section of docs/jira-sync-setup.md accordingly.
findings.finding: The rollback procedure ('pin workflow mode to dry-run') does not address the already-applied Jira mutations from the live heal (archived->Done, GC-after-grace, adopt). If the consumer flag is rolled back after heal, the Jira state changes are not reversed, creating a state mismatch between the BindingStore baseline and the live Jira records.
findings.location: AC line 5 — 'rollback (pin workflow mode to dry-run) rehearsed'; Remaining OPERATIONAL — heal of 7 known drift instances

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the BindingStore entry retirement policy (e.g., TTL-based eviction, explicit delete on ticket GC, or periodic compaction) and specify that the consumer treats a baseline older than N passes as equivalent to 'absent' (local-wins degrade path) rather than authoritative.
findings.finding: The BindingStore shared state has no specified RETIRE/cleanup path for entries corresponding to Jira tickets that are deleted, merged, or otherwise retired after the baseline is written. Over time the store will accumulate stale entries with no eviction policy, and the plan does not address staleness (distinct from 'absent' or 'corrupt') as a consumer-tolerance case.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — 're-point outbound_fields._local_matches_prev … from prev_snapshot to BindingStore.get_baseline'; consumer tolerance spec

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency safety mechanism for BindingStore (e.g., file lock, DB transaction, workflow-level mutex) and confirm that the circuit breaker cap is enforced globally across concurrent executions, not per-process.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify concurrency safety for BindingStore reads and writes when the 20-min recurring workflow could overlap with a manual heal run or a delayed prior execution. No locking, CAS, or transaction guarantee is stated for BindingStore mutations.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — workflow 'runs rebar reconcile --mode live every 20 min'; Remaining CODE — 'BindingStore.get_baseline'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the Phase-3 consumer-swap flag is a net-new `ReconcilerConfig` key distinct from `reconciler.baseline_dual_write`, name it explicitly in the plan, and verify the two flags cannot be mis-combined; or consider collapsing both into a single enum-typed `reconciler.baseline_phase` to avoid config-surface proliferation.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a second rollout-sequencing feature flag for the Phase-3 consumer swap ('behind a flag') without naming it or confirming it is distinct from the already-landed `reconciler.baseline_dual_write`. If the new flag serves the same gate as the existing one (i.e., both are true only after >=10 clean shadow passes), adding a second config key in ReconcilerConfig for the same feature train is a config-surface proliferation smell.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section: 'Phase-3 consumer swap ... behind a flag'; AC line 1: 'Phase-3 consumer-swap code merged behind a flag'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'corrupt-store fail-closed pass abort' refers to the existing prev_snapshot.json corruption guard (reconcile.py:828) or to a new BindingStore-corruption abort path that needs to be written as part of Phase-3; if the latter, add it explicitly to the code deliverable list.
findings.finding: The plan's Phase-3 consumer swap spec says 'corrupt-store fail-closed pass abort' must be preserved. The existing fail-closed abort in reconcile.py:828+ targets prev_snapshot.json corruption, not BindingStore corruption. No BindingStore corruption abort path exists in the codebase, so this claim cannot be verified as an existing behavior that the swap must preserve.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section ('corrupt-store fail-closed pass abort'); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:828

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a post-rollback schema state assertion to the rollback procedure (docs/jira-sync-setup.md §Rollback procedure and/or AC): state explicitly that after disabling the Phase-3 consumer flag, bindings.json entries retain their `baseline` sub-objects but the prev_snapshot consumer ignores them (get_baseline is never called on that code path), so the store is in a valid, safe state with no data-loss risk — and note that these keys are inert until the flag is re-enabled.
findings.finding: The plan does not assert the schema state of bindings.json AFTER rolling back the Phase-3 consumer flag. After the swap is flipped and then rolled back, bindings.json will contain `baseline` sub-objects baked in by the shadow phase. The plan says rollback = 'set flag off / keep consumer on prev_snapshot' but never states that the legacy consumer is blind to existing `baseline` keys (it is, by code design) or that the orphaned baseline data in rolled-back entries poses no DATA LOSS RISK. The rubric explicitly requires asserting the schema state post-rollback, not merely that rollback exits 0.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Consumer flag flipped in production only after the two gates above; rollback (pin workflow mode to dry-run) rehearsed.' / docs/jira-sync-setup.md §Rollback procedure
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/jira-sync-setup.md §Rollback procedure, add after step 2: 'After rollback, `bindings.json` will contain `baseline` sub-objects written during the shadow phase. These are ignored by the prev_snapshot consumer path (`get_baseline` is never invoked when the Phase-3 flag is off); absent-baseline entries continue to degrade safely to local-wins. No data is lost and no remediation of the store is required — the baseline data is inert until the flag is re-enabled. DATA LOSS RISK: none on partial failure or flag rollback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the access class and data-content posture for `decision_census` logs and the divergence alerting channel: specify what Jira fields (if any) appear in the log/alert payload, where alerts are routed (channel name/type), and whether the destination requires authentication — and confirm that GitHub Actions log verbosity is set so raw field values are masked or omitted.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `decision_census` emitted in every mode (including live) and `classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting`, but declares no access posture for either the log content or the alerting channel destination. Divergence logs are likely to contain Jira field-level data (ticket IDs, field values, classifier outcomes) needed for diagnosis; if these land in GitHub Actions stdout they are visible to all repo collaborators. The alerting channel (Slack webhook? email? PagerDuty?) is completely unspecified, so its authentication and trust level are unknown.
findings.location: Remaining CODE section — 'decision_census emitted every pass in EVERY mode + classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting in shadow (Phase-2 breadth)'; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph 'Logging & alerting security contract' section to the plan: (1) census emits aggregate counts only (no raw field values); (2) divergence alerts include ticket ID + classifier label but redact free-text fields; (3) the alert destination (e.g. `DIVERGENCE_WEBHOOK_URL`) is stored as a GitHub Actions encrypted secret, not hardcoded; (4) access to the Actions run logs is restricted to maintainer role or above.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
